content.body:
Append-only entry documenting two related refinements that shipped
together in the same session:

A. Shared GitHub project management conventions
   (commit d8bdb6e) — labels, milestones, issue/PR naming, Project v2
   fields, templates. Applied to Logbook immediately.

B. Core self-hosting scaffold
   (commit 4de6928) — .claude/ inside copilot-core so Leo can evolve
   the core in dedicated sessions instead of as a side-effect of
   project work.

Root cause is the same for both: core refinements kept landing as
side-effects of project sessions. The conventions needed a canonical
home; the scaffold makes the core a place Leo can actually work on.

Also captures: first field test of the session-wrap-up skill
(ebf685b), first full cycle of escalation-triggers (public repo push),
and a memory-level lesson about asserting tool behavior without
verification (logbook feedback memory, not a rule refinement).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
content.subject: docs(refinements): capture conventions + self-hosting from 2026-04-09 session
